children.text: Invest 90L may organize; no local threat

The National Hurricane Center has classified an area of disturbed weather in the southwestern Atlantic, Invest 90L. This is simply an area of disturbed weather that may obtain sub-tropical characteristics over the next couple days. If it is classified as a Subtropical Storm, it will be given the name Arlene. Regardless, this system will have minimal - or no - impact to the United States' east coast; maybe increased wave action. Catch the latest First Alert Forecast tonight on WTOC or at wtoc.com and in the WTOC Weather App.
Patchy, locally dense, fog possible early Monday

With higher moisture levels and a nearly calm wind, fog may develop across our region overnight. Fog is most likely between 6 and 8 a.m. across the Coastal Empire and Lowcountry; possibly becoming dense in a few spots. Outside of fog, it will be partly cloudy Monday morning.The risk of fog decreases sharply after 9 a.m. Catch the latest First Alert Forecast tonight on WTOC or at wtoc.com and in the WTOC Weather App.
